blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know. I know you're not.
She exhaled. He listened to it across two thousand miles.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: What did Ryan feel like. To you. When we were together.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You watched me be with someone else for over a year. And you said on the porch that it was the hardest thing you'd done. I'm not asking to hurt you. I'm asking because I want to understand if what I felt watching Brooke — watching that photo tonight — if that's the same thing you were living with every day.
He thought about Ryan. The hand-holding in the hall. Showing up as her friend while that was happening.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Then I'm sorry. For what that was like.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: And I'm sorry for not telling you about Cassidy straight. That was wrong.
Quiet. A different kind now — softer around the edges.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Jordan asked me more directly than I told you. He said basically the same thing your Cassidy said. Nobody's keeping score. We don't have to tell.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It wasn't even a hard no. That part I want you to know. It wasn't a close call.
They weren't all the way back. But they'd found a floor — something solid underneath the argument — and both of them knew it was still there.
